steps.title: Separate complex factors
steps.content: The sentence lists three factors after "namely." Some factors contain internal commas, such as the added detail about higher-velocity meteoroids and the example of humidity, so the main list items need stronger separation.

steps.title: Reject confusing separators
steps.content: A uses a dash, which signals elaboration rather than separating list items. B uses a comma, making the complex list harder to parse. D uses a period, ending the sentence mid-list. A semicolon correctly separates the main factors. The answer is C.
